actions.detail: role 'WC Claims Adjuster' bound to permission group
role 'WC Nurse Case Manager' bound to permission group
role 'WC Medical Bill Reviewer' bound to permission group
role 'WC Claims Manager' bound to permission group
role 'WC Compliance Auditor' bound to permission group

deferred.reason: Workers'-comp policies (no acceptance, denial, or benefit amount communicated to a claimant before the human determination; denial notices must cite the actual AOE/COE findings; no reserve change without a documented rationale; SIU referral never suspends statutory benefits or decision deadlines; pattern-of-denial / fair-claims- handling watch) require guardrail-service / OPA policy materialization, which Core does not expose as a pack surface yet. The behavioral core is partially covered today by Core invariants: every pack-configured agent is proposal-mode with four-eyes approval (agent-runtime), and the agent instructions + disposition taxonomy route final determinations exclusively to human adjusters with the WC Claims Manager as approver.

Bespoke LangGraph recipes (FNOL Intake Copilot, Compensability Clock Sentinel, Medical Bill Review Auditor, SIU Red-Flag Scorer, RTW Planner) require agent-runtime graph-module registration (BRD 23 BR-12). This pack specializes the existing case-triage + analytics agents via TenantAgentConfig prompt_params + tenant-scope RAG grounding instead.

Production read connectors (claims administration systems such as Guidewire ClaimCenter and Origami Risk; state EDI rails for IAIABC FROI/SROI first/subsequent reports of injury; medical bill-review platforms; pharmacy/PBM feeds; ISO ClaimSearch for prior-claim matching; wage/payroll systems for indemnity rate calculation) require customer credentials and pack-service connector materialization. The pack ships seed datasets in the exact landing shape; production connectors are configured through the product Data > Connections UI.

Write adapters (file FROI/SROI EDI transactions with state agencies, post accept/deny/delay determinations and reserve changes to the claims administration system, initiate indemnity benefit payments, issue statutory claimant notices) execute only behind pack-service's materialization contract and customer credentials; every such write stays proposal-mode with human approval — no autonomous benefit decision, payment, or state filing, ever.

Golden eval sets (compensability triage, deadline-risk ranking, SIU red-flag scoring, fee-schedule variance detection) require eval-service write APIs whose action names are not yet in the rbac canonical catalog (Core follow-up noted in rbac catalog.go).

Ontology registration (Claim, Claimant, Employer, Policy, BodyPart, InjuryCause, MedicalBill, Provider, ReserveChange, RTWPlan, SIUReferral, IME, ImpairmentRating, Jurisdiction, DeadlineClock, BenefitPayment) awaits pack-service; entity semantics are carried today by the wc_claims_core semantic model and dataset schemas.

Typed case schemas (fnol_intake, compensability_review, siu_investigation, medical_bill_audit, rtw_planning, litigation_management) await case-service schema materialization; today's cases carry the same fields via display_projection + the disposition taxonomy.

Model archetypes (compensability_confidence, siu_referral_score, reserve_development_risk) await experiment-service archetype APIs; the scoring capability ships as pipeline seeds instead (isolation_forest medical-bill anomaly + xgboost compensability-outcome scorer) with model-governance expectations noted in grounding memories.

Display-label overlays ("Claim", "Compensability decision due", "Deadline at risk", "SIU referred", "Above fee schedule", entity templates) require the BFF label-registry endpoints (BFF-FR-082/088) which are pack-service-tier features.
